methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $180M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
AMP Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $180M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 same-sector companies at comparable or adjacent stages, including 8 private firms (Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and others) and 1 public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base of $180M and applying a typical Series C-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x, implies a valuation of $306M. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company robotics comp set yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M (floor) to $10.0B (ceiling, driven by 1X Technologies). The peer sample includes Hadrian and Exotec at Series C and D respectively, both valued at $1.6B–$2.0B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $260M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $953M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether AMP's market position and growth trajectory align with the higher-valuation cohort (Hadrian, Exotec) or lower-capital-intensity comparables. Stage progression suggests conservatism relative to peer medians, while peer analysis alone supports substantially higher valuation. Resolution of this gap requires clarity on revenue scale, margin profile, and customer concentration relative to the comp set.
